frameworks.name: population-response manifold geometry
frameworks.one_line: decisions form along curved manifolds in neural state space

frameworks.name: competing-accumulator circuit model
frameworks.one_line: action-encoding ensembles compete with monotonic rate increases for preferred actions

frameworks.name: decision variable as partially potentiated action plan
frameworks.one_line: DV represented as graded action preparation in LIP

frameworks.name: context-dependent state-space readout
frameworks.one_line: manifolds rotate with task context, requiring task-specific readouts

core_question: Does LIP encode decision variables identically across tasks with the same competing actions, or does representational geometry change with task context?
key_assumptions: DVs in standard circuit models are partially potentiated action plans
ensembles encoding actions compete to form decisions
readout for identical competing actions should be task-invariant
manipulations: strength of supporting evidence within each task
comparison across LIP, lateral and medial prefrontal cortex
key_findings: LIP firing rates decrease with supporting evidence in face task, opposite to motion task
decisions lie on curved population manifolds misaligned with action representations
manifolds rotate in state space across task contexts
similar manifold geometry observed in lateral and medial prefrontal cortices
interpretations: DV representation geometry is task-context dependent, requiring distinct readouts
curved misaligned manifolds may be a ubiquitous motif across decision-making circuits
